name: create_draft
description:
Create a new email draft in the user's Gmail account. The draft is saved but NOT sent.
The user can review and send it from Gmail.

Use this tool to compose emails on behalf of the user. The draft-first approach ensures
the user can review the email before it is sent.

To reply to an existing email thread:
1. First use gmail_get_message to read the original message
2. Set reply_to_message_id to the original message's message_id
3. Set thread_id to the original message's thread_id
4. The subject should start with 'Re: ' followed by the original subject

After creating the draft, tell the user the draft has been saved and they can
find it in their Gmail Drafts folder to review and send.

to.description: List of recipient email addresses (To field)

cc.description: List of CC recipient email addresses (optional)

bcc.description: List of BCC recipient email addresses (optional)

body.description: Plain text body of the email

reply_to_message_id.description: Optional: message ID to reply to (creates a threaded reply). Use the message_id from gmail_get_message.

thread_id.description: Optional: thread ID to associate this draft with (for threading replies)

body_html.description: Optional: HTML body. If provided, email is sent as multipart/alternative with both plain text and HTML.

from.description: Optional: sender email address for aliases or delegated accounts. If omitted, uses the account's default address.
